message.content: Adversaries may exploit macOS Launch Daemons to achieve persistence by creating or modifying plist files that interact with the launchd service management framework. These daemons, requiring elevated privileges to install, run in the background for all users and can execute malicious payloads at system startup. Common exploitation methods include setting the RunAtLoad parameter to true and specifying a malicious executable path in the Program parameter. Adversaries may disguise these daemons using names from legitimate software to avoid detection. Poor system configurations, such as globally writable directories, can further facilitate these attacks. Notable malware families like AppleJeus, Bundlore, Dacls, Green Lambert, LoudMiner, OSX_OCEANLOTUS.D, ThiefQuest, and XCSSET have utilized Launch Daemons for persistence. Mitigation strategies include monitoring and restricting modifications to Launch Daemon plist files, ensuring proper directory permissions, and employing endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions to identify and block suspicious activities. Regular audits of system configurations and installed daemons can also help in early detection and prevention of such persistence mechanisms.
